name: PAN Card
issuingAuthority: Income Tax Department, Government of India (issued through NSDL/Protean and UTIITSL)

sections.purpose: PAN (Permanent Account Number) is a 10-digit alphanumeric code issued by the Income Tax Department. Uses: (1) Filing income tax returns — mandatory. (2) Opening bank accounts and Demat accounts. (3) Financial transactions above ₹50,000. (4) Purchase/sale of property, vehicles, jewellery above thresholds. (5) Photo identity proof — widely accepted. (6) KYC for mutual funds, insurance, and investments. (7) TDS (Tax Deducted at Source) tracking. (8) GST registration. (9) Applying for credit cards and loans.
sections.eligibility: Any Indian citizen (individual, company, firm, trust, HUF) can apply. No age limit — even minors can have PAN. No income requirement — PAN is needed regardless of whether you earn or not. Foreign nationals conducting financial transactions in India can also apply.
documents: Proof of Identity (any one): Aadhaar card / Voter ID / Passport / Driving Licence
Proof of Address (any one): Aadhaar card / Voter ID / Electricity bill / Bank statement / Passport
Proof of Date of Birth (any one): Aadhaar card / Birth certificate / Class 10 marksheet / Passport
Passport-size photographs (2 recent photos, white background)
Form 49A (for Indian citizens) or Form 49AA (for foreign nationals)
sections.fees: Physical PAN card: approximately ₹107 (Indian address delivery). PAN card with foreign address delivery: approximately ₹1,017. Reprint/Duplicate PAN: ₹107. PAN correction/change: ₹107. Instant e-PAN (via Aadhaar): FREE.
sections.processingTime: Physical PAN card: 15 to 20 working days (delivered via post). Instant e-PAN via Aadhaar: generated within minutes (available at incometax.gov.in). Online application processing: 7–10 working days for number generation. PAN card dispatch: additional 5–7 days via post.
sections.validity: PAN is permanent — it never expires. The number remains the same throughout your lifetime. Even if the physical card is lost, the PAN number remains valid in the IT Department database.
applicationSteps: Step 2 — Select 'Apply for New PAN' → Form 49A (Indian citizens)
Step 3 — Fill personal details: name, DOB, gender, father's name, address, contact details
Step 4 — Upload: photograph, signature, and supporting documents (identity, address, DOB proof)
Step 5 — Pay fee online (₹107 for Indian address)
Step 6 — Note the Acknowledgement Number for tracking
Step 7 — PAN number generated in 7–10 days (SMS/email notification)
Step 8 — Physical card dispatched via post in 15–20 days
Alternate — Instant e-PAN: Visit incometax.gov.in → Instant e-PAN → Enter Aadhaar → OTP verification → e-PAN generated in minutes (FREE, for Aadhaar holders only)
sections.instantEPan: Instant e-PAN is available FREE for Indian citizens who have Aadhaar with mobile linked. Visit incometax.gov.in → Instant e-PAN → Enter Aadhaar number → Verify with OTP → e-PAN generated in 5–10 minutes. PDF downloaded. No physical card — digital only. Can be used for all purposes where PAN is required.
sections.correction: To correct name, DOB, gender, or address on PAN: Apply at onlineservices.nsdl.com → 'Changes or Correction in existing PAN data'. Fill form, upload correct supporting document, pay ₹107. Processing: 15–20 days.
sections.lostPan: If PAN card is lost: Your PAN number remains active — only the card is lost. (1) Download e-PAN from incometax.gov.in (instant, free). (2) Apply for reprint at onlineservices.nsdl.com (₹107, delivered via post in 15–20 days). (3) If PAN number is forgotten: use 'Know Your PAN' at incometax.gov.in with name and DOB.
sections.aadhaarLinkage: PAN-Aadhaar linking is mandatory as per Income Tax Act. Link at incometax.gov.in → Link Aadhaar → Enter PAN and Aadhaar → OTP verification. Deadline extensions are announced periodically. Non-linking may result in PAN becoming 'inoperative' (cannot be used for ITR or financial transactions). Fee for late linking: ₹1000.
sections.nameCorrection: To correct name on PAN: Submit correction request at onlineservices.nsdl.com with: supporting document showing correct name (Aadhaar/Passport/Gazette notification for legal name change). Fee: ₹107. Processing: 15–20 days. Only one correction should be needed — ensure all documents match.
commonMistakes: Applying for a second PAN — duplicate PAN is illegal and attracts ₹10,000 penalty
Name mismatch between PAN application and supporting documents
Not linking PAN with Aadhaar before deadline — PAN becomes inoperative
Using agents who charge excessive fees for a simple ₹107 process
Not quoting PAN in financial transactions above ₹50,000
rejectionReasons: Duplicate PAN application detected (person already has PAN)
Document mismatch — name/DOB differs between application and proof
Photograph or signature not meeting specifications
Form filled with incorrect details (wrong category, wrong father's name)

edgeCases.title: Have two PAN numbers (duplicate)
edgeCases.problem: Person accidentally has two PAN numbers. This is illegal.
edgeCases.recommendedNextStep: Surrender the duplicate PAN immediately. Write to the Assessing Officer (AO) of your jurisdiction or apply online at onlineservices.nsdl.com → 'Request for New PAN Card or/and Changes or Correction in PAN Data' → Mention duplicate PAN to be cancelled. Keep only one active PAN. Penalty for holding duplicate PAN: ₹10,000 under Section 272B.
edgeCases.notes: Using two PANs or not surrendering duplicate can attract penalty. Act immediately if you discover a duplicate.

faqs.question: What is PAN card?
faqs.answer: PAN (Permanent Account Number) is a 10-digit alphanumeric code issued by the Income Tax Department. It is mandatory for filing tax returns, financial transactions above ₹50,000, opening bank accounts, and is widely used as photo identity proof.

faqs.question: How to apply for PAN card?
faqs.answer: Visit onlineservices.nsdl.com or pan.utiitsl.com. Fill Form 49A. Upload documents (ID, address, DOB proof) and photograph. Pay ₹107. PAN number generated in 7–10 days. Card delivered via post in 15–20 days. Alternatively, get instant free e-PAN via Aadhaar at incometax.gov.in.

faqs.question: What documents needed for PAN?
faqs.answer: Any ONE identity proof (Aadhaar/Voter ID/Passport/DL), any ONE address proof (Aadhaar/Voter ID/Electricity bill/Bank statement), any ONE DOB proof (Aadhaar/Birth certificate/10th marksheet), and 2 passport-size photographs.

faqs.question: How much does PAN card cost?
faqs.answer: Physical PAN: ₹107 (Indian address). Foreign address: ₹1,017. Reprint/duplicate: ₹107. Correction: ₹107. Instant e-PAN via Aadhaar: FREE.

faqs.question: How long does PAN card take?
faqs.answer: PAN number generated: 7–10 working days. Physical card delivery: 15–20 days via post. Instant e-PAN via Aadhaar: generated in 5–10 minutes (free).

faqs.question: Does PAN expire?
faqs.answer: No. PAN is permanent and never expires. Your PAN number remains the same throughout your lifetime regardless of address changes, name changes, or card loss.

faqs.question: How to get instant e-PAN?
faqs.answer: Visit incometax.gov.in → Instant e-PAN → Enter Aadhaar number → Verify OTP on registered mobile → e-PAN PDF generated in 5–10 minutes. Completely FREE. Requires Aadhaar with linked mobile number.

faqs.question: How to correct name on PAN?
faqs.answer: Apply at onlineservices.nsdl.com → 'Changes or Correction in existing PAN data'. Upload document showing correct name (Aadhaar/Passport/Gazette notification). Pay ₹107. Processing: 15–20 days.

faqs.question: What if I lost my PAN card?
faqs.answer: Your PAN number remains active — only the card is lost. (1) Download e-PAN free at incometax.gov.in. (2) Apply for physical reprint at onlineservices.nsdl.com for ₹107. (3) If you forgot the number: use 'Know Your PAN' at incometax.gov.in.

faqs.question: How to link PAN with Aadhaar?
faqs.answer: Visit incometax.gov.in → Link Aadhaar → Enter PAN and Aadhaar numbers → Verify with OTP. Linking is mandatory. Non-linking may make PAN 'inoperative'. Late linking fee: ₹1000.

faqs.question: Is PAN mandatory?
faqs.answer: PAN is mandatory for: filing income tax returns, transactions above ₹50,000, opening bank/Demat accounts, property purchase above ₹10 lakh, vehicle purchase, mutual fund investments, and GST registration. Penalty of ₹10,000 for not quoting PAN where required.

faqs.question: Can minor get PAN card?
faqs.answer: Yes. Minors can get PAN through their parent/guardian. The parent signs Form 49A on behalf of the minor. No separate documents from the minor are needed — parent's documents suffice. Useful when minor has income (fixed deposits, inheritance).

faqs.question: What if I have duplicate PAN?
faqs.answer: Having duplicate PAN is illegal — attracts ₹10,000 penalty. Surrender the extra PAN immediately by writing to your Assessing Officer or applying online at onlineservices.nsdl.com mentioning the PAN to cancel. Keep only one active PAN.

faqs.question: What mistakes to avoid for PAN?
faqs.answer: Avoid: applying for duplicate PAN, name mismatch in documents, not linking with Aadhaar, using unofficial agents, not quoting PAN in transactions above ₹50,000, and submitting invalid photographs.

faqs.question: Where to apply for PAN?
faqs.answer: Apply online at: onlineservices.nsdl.com (Protean — primary) or pan.utiitsl.com (UTIITSL — alternate). For instant free e-PAN: incometax.gov.in. Physical centres (TIN-FC) also available in major cities.

faqs.question: How to know my PAN number?
faqs.answer: Visit incometax.gov.in → 'Know Your PAN'. Enter name, DOB, and mobile/email. Your PAN is displayed after verification. Alternatively, PAN is printed on your Aadhaar (if linked), IT return acknowledgements, and Form 26AS.

faqs.question: What is Form 49A?
faqs.answer: Form 49A is the application form for Indian citizens/entities to apply for a new PAN or make corrections to existing PAN. It is available online at onlineservices.nsdl.com. Form 49AA is for foreign nationals.

faqs.question: What if PAN is not linked with Aadhaar?
faqs.answer: If PAN is not linked with Aadhaar after the deadline, it becomes 'inoperative' — cannot be used for ITR filing, financial transactions, or TDS claims. To reactivate: link Aadhaar and pay late fee of ₹1000. Processing: 30 days for reactivation.

faqs.question: How to track PAN application?
faqs.answer: Visit onlineservices.nsdl.com → 'Track PAN/TAN Application Status'. Enter your 15-digit Acknowledgement Number. Shows current status: processing, dispatched, or delivered.

faqs.question: What if PAN application is rejected?
faqs.answer: Reasons: duplicate PAN exists, document mismatch, photo/signature issues, or wrong form details. Check rejection reason in status tracking. Correct the issue and reapply with fresh fee (₹107). Ensure all documents match your application details exactly.
